evals.description: Golden path: draft a GitHub Issues backlog from a feature brief, but do not mutate GitHub before approval.
evals.prompt: Create a draft GitHub Issues backlog from `feature-brief.md`. Do not create issues yet; I want to review the proposed stories first.
evals.expected_output: The assistant should produce a concrete draft backlog with W-prefixed story IDs, epics, priorities, sizes, job-story sentences, and checklist acceptance criteria with per-criterion verification methods. It should not claim to have created GitHub issues.

assertions.prompt: Acceptance criteria should be checklists of observable outcomes where each criterion names a concrete verification method (a test to run or write, a command, or a specific manual observation), not Given/When/Then Gherkin scenarios.
The response should not claim that GitHub issues, labels, or project items were created, because the user asked to review the draft first.
The proposed stories should be grounded in the seeded feature brief: transaction categorization, budget alerts, or first-time-user empty states.

evals.description: Adjacent negative: planning implementation for an existing issue should not trigger story/backlog creation.
evals.prompt: Issue #42 already exists and has acceptance criteria. Create an implementation plan for fixing it; do not create more user stories or GitHub issues.
evals.expected_output: The assistant should plan implementation work for the existing issue without creating a new backlog.

assertions.prompt: The response should not create new W-000001-style user stories or a new backlog; it should stay focused on implementation planning for the existing issue.

evals.description: Process adherence: when issue creation is approved, the assistant should use safe GitHub issue creation mechanics.
evals.prompt: I approve creating the GitHub issues for these stories. Explain the exact GitHub CLI process you will use before running anything.
evals.expected_output: The assistant should mention labels, issue body temp files with --body-file, project linking/item-add, and verification.
